content.title: Ant Intelligence
content.notes: ['* aphids: small insects of a different species from ants']

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: When we think of intelligent members of the animal kingdom, the creatures that spring immediately to mind are apes and monkeys. But in fact the social lives of some members of the insect kingdom are sufficiently complex to suggest more than a hint of intelligence. Among these, the world of the ant has come in for considerable scrutiny lately, and the idea that ants demonstrate sparks of cognition has certainly not been rejected by those involved in these investigations.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Ants store food, repel attackers and use chemical signals to contact one another in case of attack. Such chemical communication can be compared to the human use of visual and auditory channels (as in religious chants, advertising images and jingles, political slogans and martial music) to arouse and propagate moods and attitudes. The biologist Lewis Thomas wrote, 'Ants are so much like human beings as to be an embarrassment. They farm fungi, raise aphids* as livestock, launch armies to war, use chemical sprays to alarm and confuse enemies, capture slaves, engage in child labour, exchange information ceaselessly. They do everything but watch television.' However, in ants there is no cultural transmission—everything must be encoded in the genes—whereas in humans the opposite is true. Only basic instincts are carried in the genes of a newborn baby; other skills being learned from others in the community as the child grows up. It may seem that this cultural continuity gives us a huge advantage over ants. They have never mastered fire nor progressed. Their fungus farming and aphid herding crafts are sophisticated when compared to the agricultural skills of humans five thousand years ago but have been totally overtaken by modern human agribusiness.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Or have they? The farming methods of ants are at least sustainable. They do not ruin environments or use enormous amounts of energy. Moreover, recent evidence suggests that the crop farming of ants may be more sophisticated and adaptable than was thought.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Ants were farmers fifty million years before humans were. Ants can't digest the cellulose in leaves—but some fungi can. The ants therefore cultivate these fungi in their nests, bringing them leaves to feed on, and then use them as a source of food. Farmer ants secrete antibiotics to control other fungi that might act as 'weeds', and spread waste to fertilise the crop.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: It was once thought that the fungus that ants cultivate was a single type that they had propagated, essentially unchanged from the distant past. Not so. Ulrich Mueller of Maryland and his colleagues genetically screened 862 different types of fungi taken from ants' nests. These turned out to be highly diverse: it seems that ants are continually domesticating new species. Even more impressively, DNA analysis of the fungi suggests that the ants improve or modify the fungi by regularly swapping and sharing strains with neighbouring ant colonies.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Whereas prehistoric man had no exposure to urban lifestyles—the forcing house of intelligence—the evidence suggests that ants have lived in urban settings for close on a hundred million years, developing and maintaining underground cities of specialised chambers and tunnels.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: When we survey Mexico City, Tokyo, Los Angeles, we are amazed at what has been accomplished by humans. Yet Hölldobler and Wilson's magnificent work for ant lovers, The Ants, describes a supercolony of the ant Formica yessensis on the Ishikari Coast of Hokkaido. This 'megalopolis' was reported to be composed of 360 million workers and a million queens living in 4,500 interconnected nests across a territory of 2.7 square kilometres.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Such enduring and intricately meshed levels of technical achievement outstrip by far anything achieved by our distant ancestors. We hail as masterpieces the cave paintings in southern France and elsewhere, dating back some 20,000 years. Ant societies existed in something like their present form more than seventy million years ago. Beside this, prehistoric man looks technologically primitive. Is this then some kind of intelligence, albeit of a different kind?

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: Research conducted at Oxford, Sussex and Zürich Universities has shown that when desert ants return from a foraging trip, they navigate by integrating bearings and distances, which they continuously update in their heads. They combine the evidence of visual landmarks with a mental library of local directions, all within a framework which is consulted and updated. So ants can learn too.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: And in a twelve-year programme of work, Ryabko and Reznikova have found evidence that ants can transmit very complex messages. Scouts who had located food in a maze returned to mobilise their foraging teams. They engaged in contact sessions, at the end of which the scout was removed in order to observe what her team might do. Often the foragers proceeded to the exact spot in the maze where the food had been. Elaborate precautions were taken to prevent the foraging team using odour clues. Discussion now centres on whether the route through the maze is communicated as a 'left-right' sequence of turns or as a 'compass bearing and distance' message.

paragraphs.label: K
paragraphs.text: During the course of this exhaustive study, Reznikova has grown so attached to her laboratory ants that she feels she knows them as individuals—even without the paint spots used to mark them. It's no surprise that Edward Wilson, in his essay, 'In the company of ants', advises readers who ask what to do with the ants in their kitchen to: 'Watch where you step. Be careful of little lives.'

groups.desc:
Questions 1-6

Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 1?
Choose
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text,
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information,
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

questions.title: Ants use the same channels of communication as humans do.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“same channels of communication”意思是“相同的交流渠道”，而原文中提到蚂蚁使用的是“chemical signals”（化学信号），并将其与人类使用的“visual and auditory channels”（视觉和听觉渠道）进行比较。这里的“compared to”表示的是比较，而不是相同。因此，题干中的信息与原文不符，正确答案是 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: Such chemical communication can be compared to the human use of visual and auditory channels (as in religious chants, advertising images and jingles, political slogans and martial music) to arouse and propagate moods and attitudes.
central_sentences.translation: 这种化学交流可以与人类使用视觉和听觉渠道（如宗教颂歌、广告图像和歌曲、政治口号和军乐）来唤起和传播情绪和态度进行比较。

target_sen.target_sen: Ants store food, repel attackers and use chemical signals to contact one another in case of attack.

Such chemical communication can be compared to the human use of visual and auditory channels (as in religious chants, advertising images and jingles, political slogans and martial music) to arouse and propagate moods and attitudes.

questions.title: City life is one factor that encourages the development of intelligence.
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是 TRUE，因为文章中明确提到城市生活（urban lifestyles）是促进智力发展的因素（the forcing house of intelligence）。通过识别关键词和同义替换，即使词汇量有限的学生也能找到正确答案。学生容易错选 NOT GIVEN，但实际上文章中有明确的信息。

central_sentences.sentence: Whereas prehistoric man had no exposure to urban lifestyles - the forcing house of intelligence - the evidence suggests that ants have lived in urban settings for close on a hundred million years, developing and maintaining underground cities of specialised chambers and tunnels.
central_sentences.translation: 而史前人类并未接触城市生活方式——智力发展的催化剂——证据表明蚂蚁已经在城市环境中生活了近一亿年，发展并维护着由专门的腔室和隧道组成的地下城市。

target_sen.target_sen: Whereas prehistoric man had no exposure to urban lifestyles - the forcing house of intelligence - the evidence suggests that ants have lived in urban settings for close on a hundred million years, developing and maintaining underground cities of specialised chambers and tunnels.

Whereas prehistoric man had no exposure to urban lifestyles - the forcing house of intelligence - the evidence suggests that ants have lived in urban settings for close on a hundred million years, developing and maintaining underground cities of specialised chambers and tunnels.

questions.title: Ants can build large cities more quickly than humans do.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“Ants can build large cities more quickly than humans do”在文章中找不到对应的内容。虽然文章提到蚂蚁建造了复杂的地下城市和超级殖民地，但没有提到建造速度的比较。因此，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: Whereas prehistoric man had no exposure to urban lifestyles - the forcing house of intelligence - the evidence suggests that ants have lived in urban settings for close on a hundred million years, developing and maintaining underground cities of specialised chambers and tunnels.
central_sentences.translation: 虽然史前人类没有接触过城市生活方式——这是智力的催化剂——但证据表明，蚂蚁已经在城市环境中生活了近一亿年，发展和维护着由专门的房间和隧道组成的地下城市。

target_sen.target_sen: Whereas prehistoric man had no exposure to urban lifestyles - the forcing house of intelligence - the evidence suggests that ants have lived in urban settings for close on a hundred million years, developing and maintaining underground cities of specialised chambers and tunnels.

questions.title: Some ants can find their way by making calculations based on distance and position.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息 "Some ants can find their way by making calculations based on distance and position." 可以在文章中找到对应的内容。文章中提到 "desert ants return from a foraging trip, they navigate by integrating bearings and distances, which they continuously update in their heads." 这说明蚂蚁通过计算方向和距离来导航。因此，题干中的信息在文章中得到了明确的表达，答案是TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Research conducted at Oxford, Sussex and Zürich Universities has shown that when desert ants return from a foraging trip, they navigate by integrating bearings and distances, which they continuously update in their heads.
central_sentences.translation: 牛津大学、萨塞克斯大学和苏黎世大学进行的研究表明，当沙漠蚂蚁从觅食旅程返回时，它们通过在头脑中不断更新方位和距离的整合来导航。

target_sen.target_sen: Research conducted at Oxford, Sussex and Zürich Universities has shown that when desert ants return from a foraging trip, they navigate by integrating bearings and distances, which they continuously update in their heads.

Research conducted at Oxford, Sussex and Zürich Universities has shown that when desert ants return from a foraging trip, they navigate by integrating bearings and distances, which they continuously update in their heads.

Research conducted at Oxford, Sussex and Zürich Universities has shown that when desert ants return from a foraging trip, they navigate by integrating bearings and distances, which they continuously update in their heads.

Research conducted at Oxford, Sussex and Zürich Universities has shown that when desert ants return from a foraging trip, they navigate by integrating bearings and distances, which they continuously update in their heads.

questions.title: In one experiment, foraging teams were able to use their sense of smell to find food.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“觅食队伍能够使用嗅觉找到食物”，而原文明确提到“采取了详细的预防措施，防止觅食队伍使用气味线索”。这与题干信息相反，因此答案是FALSE。题干中的“foraging teams”对应原文的“foraging team”，“sense of smell”对应“odour clues”，“find food”对应“located food”。通过这些对应关系，可以确定题干信息与原文信息相反。

central_sentences.sentence: Elaborate precautions were taken to prevent the foraging team using odour clues.
central_sentences.translation: 采取了详细的预防措施，以防止觅食队伍使用气味线索。

target_sen.target_sen: Scouts who had located food in a maze returned to mobilise their foraging teams.
Elaborate precautions were taken to prevent the foraging team using odour clues.
Elaborate precautions were taken to prevent the foraging team using odour clues.
Scouts who had located food in a maze returned to mobilise their foraging teams.

questions.title: The essay, 'In the company of ants', explores ant communication.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“《In the company of ants》这篇文章探讨了蚂蚁的交流”，而原文中提到这篇文章的内容是关于如何对待厨房里的蚂蚁，并没有提到蚂蚁的交流。因此，题干中的信息在文章中没有被明确提到，所以答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: It's no surprise that Edward Wilson, in his essay, 'In the company of ants', advises readers who ask what to do with the ants in their kitchen to: 'Watch where you step. Be careful of little lives.'
central_sentences.translation: 爱德华·威尔逊在他的文章《与蚂蚁为伍》中建议询问如何处理厨房里蚂蚁的读者："注意脚下。小心那些小生命。"这并不令人惊讶。

target_sen.target_sen: It's no surprise that Edward Wilson, in his essay, 'In the company of ants', advises readers who ask what to do with the ants in their kitchen to: 'Watch where you step.
It's no surprise that Edward Wilson, in his essay, 'In the company of ants', advises readers who ask what to do with the ants in their kitchen to: 'Watch where you step.

groups.title: Ants as farmers
groups.desc:
Questions 7-13
Complete the summary using the list of words, A-O, below.
Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.
groups.collect: Ants have sophisticated methods of farming, including herding livestock and growing crops, which are in many ways similar to those used in human agriculture. The ants cultivate a large number of different species of edible fungi which convert $1796$into a form which they can digest. They use their own natural $1797$as weed-killers and also use unwanted materials as$1798$. Genetic analysis shows they constantly upgrade these fungi by developing new species and by $1799$species with neighbouring ant colonies. In fact, the farming methods of ants could be said to be more advanced than human agribusiness, since they use $1800$methods, they do not affect the $1801$and do not waste $1802$.

list.text: aphids
agricultural
cellulose
exchanging
energy
fertilizers
food
fungi
growing
interbreeding
natural
other species
secretions
sustainable
environment

questions.analysis: 题干中的“convert...into a form which they can digest”对应文章中的“can't digest the cellulose in leaves - but some fungi can”，所以最直接的对应关系是“cellulose”。

central_sentences.sentence: Ants can't digest the cellulose in leaves - but some fungi can.
central_sentences.translation: 蚂蚁不能消化叶子中的纤维素——但一些真菌可以。

target_sen.target_sen: Ants can't digest the cellulose in leaves - but some fungi can.
Ants can't digest the cellulose in leaves - but some fungi can.
The ants therefore cultivate these fungi in their nests, bringing them leaves to feed on, and then use them as a source of food.
Farmer ants secrete antibiotics to control other fungi that might act as 'weeds', and spread waste to fertilise the crop.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“natural”对应文章中的“secrete”，“weed-killers”对应“control other fungi that might act as 'weeds'”，“unwanted materials”对应“waste”，“fertilizers”对应“fertilise”。因此，正确答案是“secretions”。

central_sentences.sentence: Farmer ants secrete antibiotics to control other fungi that might act as 'weeds', and spread waste to fertilise the crop.
central_sentences.translation: 农夫蚂蚁分泌抗生素来控制可能充当"杂草"的其他真菌，并且散布废物来为作物施肥。

target_sen.target_sen: Farmer ants secrete antibiotics to control other fungi that might act as 'weeds', and spread waste to fertilise the crop.

syn_ary.synonym: control other fungi that might act as 'weeds'

target_sen.target_sen: Farmer ants secrete antibiotics to control other fungi that might act as 'weeds', and spread waste to fertilise the crop.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "use unwanted materials as【 】" 对应文章中的 "spread waste to fertilise the crop"。其中 "unwanted materials" 对应 "waste"，"use" 对应 "spread"，而 "fertilizers" 对应 "fertilise"。因此，正确答案是 "fertilizers"。

central_sentences.sentence: Farmer ants secrete antibiotics to control other fungi that might act as 'weeds', and spread waste to fertilise the crop.
central_sentences.translation: 农夫蚂蚁分泌抗生素来控制可能充当"杂草"的其他真菌，并且传播废物来为作物施肥。

target_sen.target_sen: Farmer ants secrete antibiotics to control other fungi that might act as 'weeds', and spread waste to fertilise the crop.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“developing new species”和“neighbouring ant colonies”在原文中分别对应“domesticating new species”和“neighbouring ant colonies”。原文中提到蚂蚁通过“swapping and sharing”来改进真菌，这与题干中的“by 【 】species with neighbouring ant colonies”相对应。因此，最合适的答案是“exchanging”，因为它与“swapping and sharing”意思相同。

central_sentences.sentence: Even more impressively, DNA analysis of the fungi suggests that the ants improve or modify the fungi by regularly swapping and sharing strains with neighbouring ant colonies.
central_sentences.translation: 更令人印象深刻的是，对真菌的DNA分析表明，蚂蚁通过定期与邻近的蚁群交换和共享菌株来改善或修改真菌。

target_sen.target_sen: Even more impressively, DNA analysis of the fungi suggests that the ants improve or modify the fungi by regularly swapping and sharing strains with neighbouring ant colonies.
Even more impressively, DNA analysis of the fungi suggests that the ants improve or modify the fungi by regularly swapping and sharing strains with neighbouring ant colonies.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“methods”在原文中直接对应“methods”，而“more advanced”在原文中对应“more sophisticated”。通过这些线索词，我们可以定位到原文中的句子：“The farming methods of ants are at least sustainable. They do not ruin environments or use enormous amounts of energy.” 这句话明确指出蚂蚁的耕作方法是“sustainable”，即“可持续的”，因此答案是`sustainable`。

central_sentences.sentence: The farming methods of ants are at least sustainable. They do not ruin environments or use enormous amounts of energy.
central_sentences.translation: 蚂蚁的耕作方法至少是可持续的。它们不会破坏环境，也不会消耗大量能源。

target_sen.target_sen: The farming methods of ants are at least sustainable.
They do not ruin environments or use enormous amounts of energy.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "do not affect the 【 】" 对应文章中的 "do not ruin environments"。因此，最直接的答案是 "environment"。文章明确指出蚂蚁的耕作方法不会破坏环境，这与题干的描述完全一致。

central_sentences.sentence: The farming methods of ants are at least sustainable. They do not ruin environments or use enormous amounts of energy.
central_sentences.translation: 蚂蚁的耕作方法至少是可持续的。它们不会破坏环境，也不会使用大量能源。

target_sen.target_sen: They do not ruin environments or use enormous amounts of energy.

questions.analysis: 根据文章内容，蚂蚁的耕作方法是可持续的，不会破坏环境，也不会浪费能源。因此，题干中的 "do not waste [ ]" 对应的正确答案是 "energy"。文章中明确提到 "do not use enormous amounts of energy"，这与题干中的 "do not waste" 是同义表达。

central_sentences.sentence: The farming methods of ants are at least sustainable. They do not ruin environments or use enormous amounts of energy.
central_sentences.translation: 蚂蚁的耕作方法至少是可持续的。它们不会破坏环境，也不会消耗大量能源。

content.title: Population movements and genetics
content.summary: {'A': '这段话主要讲述了人类种群起源和分布的研究过去基于考古和化石证据，但自20世纪50年代以来，一些技术的发展使得这些主题的研究更加可靠和客观，现在最好的早期人口迁移信息来自于“活体考古学”，即遗传物质中的线索。', 'B': '最近的研究表明，通过对遗传学的研究，我们可以更好地了解人类首次进入美洲的时间和方式。', 'C': '这段话主要讲了一个由生物人类学家罗伯特·威廉姆斯领导的重要项目，该项目关注人类血液中一种特定蛋白质免疫球蛋白G的变异体（称为Gm同种型）。通过比较两个不同人群（例如两个印度部落）的Gm同种型，可以确定它们的遗传“距离”，从而间接推测这些人群上次杂交的时间。', 'D': '这段话主要讲述了威廉姆斯和他的同事在二十年的时间里对5000多名美洲印第安人的血液进行了采样，并发现他们的Gm等位基因可以分为两组，其中一组与中美洲和南美洲印第安人的遗传类型相对应。其他测试表明，因纽特人（或爱斯基摩人）和阿留申人形成了第三个群体。根据这些证据，可以推断出在白令海峡地区有三次主要的迁徙浪潮。第一次迁徙发生在15000多年前，是所有中美洲和南美洲印第安人的祖先。第二次迁徙发生在大约14000-12000年前，带来了纳-德内猎人，他们是纳瓦霍和阿帕奇的祖先（他们只在大约600或700年前从加拿大南迁）。第三次迁徙发生在大约10000或9000年前，是从东北亚迁徙来的群体，是现代爱斯基摩人和阿留申人的祖先。', 'E': '这段话主要讲述了遗传学家道格拉斯·华莱士通过研究三个广泛分布的美洲原住民群体的线粒体DNA，发现这三个群体都是源自同一个祖先（古印第安人）的后代，与罗伯特·威廉姆斯的研究结果相符合。\n', 'F': '这段话主要讲述了两种研究方法，即牙齿和语言研究，对于美洲原住民人口的起源提供了一些线索。通过对牙齿的分析，生物人类学家克里斯蒂·特纳认为牙冠和牙根具有很高的遗传成分，几乎不受环境和其他因素的影响。特纳对许多新旧世界的古代和现代标本进行的研究表明，大多数史前美洲人与北亚人群有着牙冠和牙根特征的联系，例如切牙铲状（牙齿表面的一个或两个凹陷）、上颌第一前磨牙单根和下颌第一臼齿三根根。特纳认为，这与单一的古印第安人从北亚迁徙的观点相吻合，他通过校准牙齿微进化的速率将其定在14,000年前之前。牙齿分析还表明，后来还有两次纳德内斯人和爱斯基摩-阿留申人的迁徙。', 'G': '这段话主要讲述了语言学家Joseph Greenberg自1950年代以来一直主张所有美洲原住民语言属于一个“美洲印第安”家族，除了纳-德内语和爱斯基摩-阿留申语系，这一观点支持了三个主要迁徙的想法。尽管Greenberg在语言学界中占少数，大多数学者更倾向于认为美洲印第安人曾经有多次大规模迁徙来解释一度有超过1000种语言存在的现象，但新的遗传和牙齿证据无疑为Greenberg的观点提供了强有力的支持。然而，对于迁徙的日期应该谨慎对待，除非有硬性的考古证据支持。'}
content.notes: ['1 New World: the American continent, as opposed to the so-called Old World of Europe, Asia and Africa', '2 modern Native American: an American descended from the groups that were native to America', '3 Inuit and Aleut: two of the ethnic groups native to the northern regions of North America (i.e. northern Canada and Greenland)', '4 DNA: the substance in which genetic information is stored', '5 crown/root: parts of the tooth', '6 incisor/premolar/molar: kinds of teeth']

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Study of the origins and distribution of human populations used to be based on archaeological and fossil evidence. A number of techniques developed since the 1950s, however, have placed the study of these subjects on a sounder and more objective footing. The best information on early population movements is now being obtained from the 'archaeology of the living body', the clues to be found in genetic material.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Recent work on the problem of when people first entered the Americas is an example of the value of these new techniques. North-east Asia and Siberia have long been accepted as the launching ground for the first human colonisers of the New World 1. But was there one major wave of migration across the Bering Strait into the Americas, or several? And when did this event, or events, take place? In recent years, new clues have come from research into genetics, including the distribution of genetic markers in modern Native Americans 2

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: An important project, led by the biological anthropologist Robert Williams, focused on the variants (called Gm allotypes) of one particular protein immunoglobulin G - found in the fluid portion of human blood. All proteins 'drift', or produce variants, over the generations, and members of an interbreeding human population will share a set of such variants. Thus, by comparing the Gm allotypes of two different populations (e.g. two Indian tribes), one can establish their genetic 'distance', which itself can be calibrated to give an indication of the length of time since these populations last interbred.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Williams and his colleagues sampled the blood of over 5,000 American Indians in western North America during a twenty-year period. They found that their Gm allotypes could be divided into two groups, one of which also corresponded to the genetic typing of Central and South American Indians. Other tests showed that the Inuit (or Eskimo) and Aleut 3 formed a third group. From this evidence it was deduced that there had been three major waves of migration across the Bering Strait. The first, Paleo-Indian, wave more than 15,000 years ago was ancestral to all Central and South American Indians. The second wave, about 14,000-12,000 years ago, brought Na-Dene hunters, ancestors of the Navajo and Apache (who only migrated south from Canada about 600 or 700 years ago). The third wave, perhaps 10,000 or 9,000 years ago, saw the migration from North-east Asia of groups ancestral to the modern Eskimo and Aleut.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: How far does other research support these conclusions? Geneticist Douglas Wallace has studied mitochondrial DNA 4 in blood samples from three widely separated Native American groups: Pima-Papago Indians in Arizona, Maya Indians on the Yucatán peninsula, Mexico, and Ticuna Indians in the Upper Amazon region of Brazil. As would have been predicted by Robert Williams's work, all three groups appear to be descended from the same ancestral (Paleo-Indian) population.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text:
There are two other kinds of research that have thrown some light on the origins of the Native American population; they involve the study of teeth and of languages. The biological anthropologist Christy Turner is an expert in the analysis of changing physical characteristics in human teeth. He argues that tooth crowns and roots 5 have a high genetic component, minimally affected by environmental and other factors. Studies carried out by Turner of many thousands of New and Old World specimens, both ancient and modern, suggest that the majority of prehistoric Americans are linked to Northern Asian populations by crown and root traits such as incisor 6 shoveling (a scooping out on one or both surfaces of the tooth), single-rooted upper first premolars 6 and triple-rooted lower first molars 6.
According to Turner, this ties in with the idea of a single Paleo-Indian migration out of North Asia, which he sets at before 14,000 years ago by calibrating rates of dental micro-evolution. Tooth analyses also suggest that there were two later migrations of Na-Denes and Eskimo-Aleut.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: The linguist Joseph Greenberg has, since the 1950s, argued that all Native American languages belong to a single 'Amerind' family, except for Na-Dene and Eskimo-Aleut - a view that gives credence to the idea of three main migrations. Greenberg is in a minority among fellow linguists, most of whom favour the notion of a great many waves of migration to account for the more than 1,000 languages spoken at one time by American Indians. But there is no doubt that the new genetic and dental evidence provides strong backing for Greenberg's view. Dates given for the migrations should nevertheless be treated with caution, except where supported by hard archaeological evidence.

central_sentences.sentence: Thus, by comparing the Gm allotypes of two different populations (e.g. two Indian tribes), one can establish their genetic 'distance', which itself can be calibrated to give an indication of the length of time since these populations last interbred.

target_sen.target_sen: An important project, led by the biological anthropologist Robert Williams, focused on the variants (called Gm allotypes) of one particular protein immunoglobin G - found in the fluid portion of human blood.

questions.analysis: 这部分文章的重点是通过对血液样本的研究，得出了关于三次主要迁徙波的结论。小标题i中的“results of the research into blood-variants”与文章中的“deduced”和“Gm allotypes”直接对应，说明了研究结果。因此，Section D对应的小标题是i。

central_sentences.sentence: Williams and his colleagues sampled the blood of over 5,000 American Indians in western North America during a twenty-year period. They found that their Gm allotypes could be divided into two groups, one of which also corresponded to the genetic typing of Central and South American Indians. Other tests showed that the Inuit (or Eskimo) and Aleut 3 formed a third group. From this evidence it was deduced that there had been three major waves of migration across the Bering Strait. The

questions.analysis: 根据文章内容，Williams的研究表明有三次主要的迁徙波次。题目中提到的【Q7008_101001】单元格对应的迁徙路线是第三次迁徙，即“perhaps 10,000 or 9,000 years ago, saw the migration from North-east Asia of groups ancestral to the modern Eskimo and Aleut”。根据地图标注，第三次迁徙路线是E。因此，正确答案是E。

The first, Paleo-Indian, wave more than 15,000 years ago was ancestral to all Central and South American Indians. The second wave, about 14,000-12,000 years ago, brought Na-Dene hunters, ancestors of the Navajo and Apache (who only migrated south from Canada about 600 or 700 years ago). The third wave, perhaps 10,000 or 9,000 years ago, saw the migration from North-east Asia of groups ancestral to the modern Eskimo and Aleut.
central_sentences.translation: 从这些证据可以推断出，有三次主要的迁徙浪潮穿过白令海峡。第一次是15,000多年前的古印第安人浪潮，是所有中美洲和南美洲印第安人的祖先。第二次浪潮发生在约14,000-12,000年前，带来了纳-德内猎人，他们是纳瓦霍人和阿帕奇人的祖先（这些人大约600或700年前才从加拿大南迁）。第三次浪潮可能发生在10,000或9,000年前，是现代爱斯基摩人和阿留申人祖先从东北亚的迁徙。

questions.title: Christy Turner's research involved the examination of

options.text: teeth from both prehistoric and modern Americans and Asians.

dental specimens from the majority of prehistoric Americans.
the eating habits of American and Asian populations.

questions.analysis: Christy Turner's研究涉及对史前和现代美洲人和亚洲人的牙齿进行分析。文章中明确提到Turner研究了“新旧大陆标本，包括古代和现代”，这与选项A中的“史前和现代美洲人和亚洲人的牙齿”相对应。因此，选项A是正确的。

central_sentences.sentence: Studies carried out by Turner of many thousands of New and Old World specimens, both ancient and modern, suggest that the majority of prehistoric Americans are linked to Northern Asian populations by crown and root traits such as incisor 6 shoveling (a scooping out on one or both surfaces of the tooth), single-rooted upper first premolars 6
central_sentences.translation: Turner对数千个新旧大陆的古代和现代样本进行的研究表明，大多数史前美洲人通过牙冠和牙根特征（如铲形切牙、单根上第一前磨牙和三根下第一磨牙）与北亚人群有联系。

target_sen.target_sen: Studies carried out by Turner of many thousands of New and Old World specimens, both ancient and modern, suggest that the majority of prehistoric Americans are linked to Northern Asian populations by crown and root traits such as incisor 6 shoveling (a scooping out on one or both surfaces of the tooth), single-rooted upper first premolars 6 and triple-rooted lower first molars 6.
The biological anthropologist Christy Turner is an expert in the analysis of changing physical characteristics in human teeth.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Forests are one of the main elements of our natural heritage. The decline of Europe's forests over the last decade and a half has led to an increasing awareness and understanding of the serious imbalances which threaten them. European countries are becoming increasingly concerned by major threats to European forests, threats which know no frontiers other than those of geography or climate: air pollution, soil deterioration, the increasing number of forest fires and sometimes even the mismanagement of our woodland and forest heritage. There has been a growing awareness of the need for countries to get together to co-ordinate their policies. In December 1990, Strasbourg hosted the first Ministerial Conference on the protection of Europe's forests. The conference brought together 31 countries from both Western and Eastern Europe. The topics discussed included the co-ordinated study of the destruction of forests, as well as how to combat forest fires and the extension of European research programs on the forest ecosystem. The preparatory work for the conference had been undertaken at two meetings of experts. Their initial task was to decide which of the many forest problems of concern to Europe involved the largest number of countries and might be the subject of joint action. Those confined to particular geographical areas, such as countries bordering the Mediterranean or the Nordic countries therefore had to be discarded. However, this does not mean that in future they will be ignored.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: As a whole, European countries see forests as performing a triple function: biological, economic and recreational. The first is to act as a 'green lung' for our planet; by means of photosynthesis, forests produce oxygen through the transformation of solar energy, thus fulfilling what for humans is the essential role of an immense, non-polluting power plant. At the same time, forests provide raw materials for human activities through their constantly renewed production of wood. Finally, they offer those condemned to spend five days a week in an urban environment an unrivalled area of freedom to unwind and take part in a range of leisure activities, such as hunting, riding and hiking. The economic importance of forests has been understood since the dawn of man - wood was the first fuel. The other aspects have been recognised only for a few centuries but they are becoming more and more important. Hence, there is a real concern throughout Europe about the damage to the forest environment which threatens these three basic roles.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: The myth of the 'natural' forest has survived, yet there are effectively no remaining 'primary' forests in Europe. All European forests are artificial, having been adapted and exploited by man for thousands of years. This means that a forest policy is vital, that it must transcend national frontiers and generations of people, and that it must allow for the inevitable changes that take place in the forests, in needs, and hence in policy. The Strasbourg conference was one of the first events on such a scale to reach this conclusion. A general declaration was made that 'a central place in any ecologically coherent forest policy must be given to continuity over time and to the possible effects of unforeseen events, to ensure that the full potential of these forests is maintained'.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: That general declaration was accompanied by six detailed resolutions to assist national policy-making. The first proposes the extension and systematisation of surveillance sites to monitor forest decline. Forest decline is still poorly understood but leads to the loss of a high proportion of a tree's needles or leaves. The entire continent and the majority of species are now affected: between 30% and 50% of the tree population. The condition appears to result from the cumulative effect of a number of factors, with atmospheric pollutants the principal culprits. Compounds of nitrogen and sulphur dioxide should be particularly closely watched. However, their effects are probably accentuated by climatic factors, such as drought and hard winters, or soil imbalances such as soil acidification, which damages the roots. The second resolution concentrates on the need to preserve the genetic diversity of European forests. The aim is to reverse the decline in the number of tree species or at least to preserve the 'genetic material' of all of them. Although forest fires do not affect all of Europe to the same extent, the amount of damage caused the experts to propose as the third resolution that the Strasbourg conference consider the establishment of a European databank on the subject. All information used in the development of national preventative policies would become generally available. The subject of the fourth resolution discussed by the ministers was mountain forests. In Europe, it is undoubtedly the mountain ecosystem which has changed most rapidly and is most at risk. A thinly scattered permanent population and development of leisure activities, particularly skiing, have resulted in significant long-term changes to the local ecosystems. Proposed developments include a preferential research program on mountain forests. The fifth resolution relaunched the European research network on the physiology of trees, called Eurosilva. Eurosilva should support joint European research on tree diseases and their physiological and biochemical aspects. Each country concerned could increase the number of scholarships and other financial support for doctoral theses and research projects in this area. Finally, the conference established the framework for a European research network on forest ecosystems. This would also involve harmonising activities in individual countries as well as identifying a number of priority research topics relating to the protection of forests. The Strasbourg conference's main concern was to provide for the future. This was the initial motivation, one now shared by all 31 participants representing 31 European countries. Their final text commits them to on-going discussion between government representatives with responsibility for forests.

questions.title: Forest problems of Mediterranean countries are to be discussed at the next meeting of experts.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“Forest problems of Mediterranean countries are to be discussed at the next meeting of experts”在文章中没有明确提到。虽然文章提到地中海国家的森林问题在这次会议中被排除在外，但未来并不意味着会被忽视。然而，文章并没有明确提到这些问题会在下一次专家会议上讨论。因此，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

target_sen.target_sen: Their initial task was to decide which of the many forest problems of concern to Europe involved the largest number of countries and might be the subject of joint action.
Those confined to particular geographical areas, such as countries bordering the Mediterranean or the Nordic countries therefore had to be discarded.
However, this does not mean that in future they will be ignored.

central_sentences.sentence: Those confined to particular geographical areas, such as countries bordering the Mediterranean or the Nordic countries therefore had to be discarded.
central_sentences.translation: 那些局限于特定地理区域的问题，如地中海沿岸国家或北欧国家的问题，因此必须被排除。

target_sen.target_sen: Those confined to particular geographical areas, such as countries bordering the Mediterranean or the Nordic countries therefore had to be discarded.
Those confined to particular geographical areas, such as countries bordering the Mediterranean or the Nordic countries therefore had to be discarded.

questions.title: Forests are a renewable source of raw material.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Forests are a renewable source of raw material" 意思是 "森林是可再生的原材料来源"。在原文中，"forests provide raw materials for human activities through their constantly renewed production of wood" 这句话明确指出森林通过不断再生的木材生产提供原材料。这里的 "constantly renewed" 对应题干中的 "renewable"，"raw materials" 对应 "raw material"。因此，题干信息在文章中找到了对应，答案为 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: At the same time, forests provide raw materials for human activities through their constantly renewed production of wood.
central_sentences.translation: 同时，森林通过不断更新的木材生产为人类活动提供原材料。

target_sen.target_sen: At the same time, forests provide raw materials for human activities through their constantly renewed production of wood.
At the same time, forests provide raw materials for human activities through their constantly renewed production of wood.

questions.title: The biological functions of forests were recognised only in the twentieth century.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“森林的生物功能仅在20世纪才被认识到”，而原文中明确提到“其他方面（包括生物功能）仅在几个世纪前才被认识到”。“几个世纪”明显早于20世纪，因此题干中的信息是错误的，正确答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: The other aspects have been recognised only for a few centuries but they are becoming more and more important.
central_sentences.translation: 其他方面（森林的功能）仅在几个世纪前才被认识到，但它们正变得越来越重要。

target_sen.target_sen: The other aspects have been recognised only for a few centuries but they are becoming more and more important.
The other aspects have been recognised only for a few centuries but they are becoming more and more important.
The other aspects have been recognised only for a few centuries but they are becoming more and more important.

questions.title: Natural forests still exist in parts of Europe.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Natural forests still exist in parts of Europe”意思是“天然森林在欧洲的一些地方仍然存在”。原文中明确提到“there are effectively no remaining 'primary' forests in Europe”，即“实际上欧洲没有剩余的‘原始’森林”。这与题干的意思正好相反，因此答案为FALSE。通过找到题干中的线索词“Natural forests”、“exist”和“parts of Europe”，并在原文中找到对应的同义替换词，可以确定答案句并得出正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: The myth of the 'natural' forest has survived, yet there are effectively no remaining 'primary' forests in Europe.
central_sentences.translation: "天然"森林的神话仍然存在，但实际上欧洲已经没有剩余的"原始"森林了。

target_sen.target_sen: The myth of the 'natural' forest has survived, yet there are effectively no remaining 'primary' forests in Europe.
The myth of the 'natural' forest has survived, yet there are effectively no remaining 'primary' forests in Europe.
The myth of the 'natural' forest has survived, yet there are effectively no remaining 'primary' forests in Europe.
The myth of the 'natural' forest has survived, yet there are effectively no remaining 'primary' forests in Europe.
All European forests are artificial, having been adapted and exploited by man for thousands of years.

questions.title: Forest policy should be limited by national boundaries.
questions.analysis: 题干陈述“森林政策应该被国家边界限制”，而原文明确指出森林政策应当超越国家边界。因此，题干的陈述与原文内容相反，答案是 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: This means that a forest policy is vital, that it must transcend national frontiers and generations of people, and that it must allow for the inevitable changes that take place in the forests, in needs, and hence in policy.
central_sentences.translation: 这意味着森林政策至关重要，它必须超越国家边界和几代人，并且必须考虑到森林、需求以及由此产生的政策中不可避免的变化。

target_sen.target_sen: This means that a forest policy is vital, that it must transcend national frontiers and generations of people, and that it must allow for the inevitable changes that take place in the forests, in needs, and hence in policy.

This means that a forest policy is vital, that it must transcend national frontiers and generations of people, and that it must allow for the inevitable changes that take place in the forests, in needs, and hence in policy.

questions.title: The Strasbourg conference decided that a forest policy must allow for the possibility of change.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“Strasbourg conference decided that a forest policy must allow for the possibility of change”在原文中找到了对应的表达：“a forest policy ... must allow for the inevitable changes”。这表明斯特拉斯堡会议决定森林政策必须考虑到变化的可能性。因此，题干信息在文章中得到了明确的支持，答案为TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: This means that a forest policy is vital, that it must transcend national frontiers and generations of people, and that it must allow for the inevitable changes that take place in the forests, in needs, and hence in policy.
central_sentences.translation: 这意味着森林政策是至关重要的，它必须超越国界和代际，并且必须考虑到森林中、需求中以及因此而产生的政策中不可避免的变化。

target_sen.target_sen: The Strasbourg conference was one of the first events on such a scale to reach this conclusion.

This means that a forest policy is vital, that it must transcend national frontiers and generations of people, and that it must allow for the inevitable changes that take place in the forests, in needs, and hence in policy.

A general declaration was made that 'a central place in any ecologically coherent forest policy must be given to continuity over time and to the possible effects of unforeseen events, to ensure that the full potential of these forests is maintained'.

This means that a forest policy is vital, that it must transcend national frontiers and generations of people, and that it must allow for the inevitable changes that take place in the forests, in needs, and hence in policy.

This means that a forest policy is vital, that it must transcend national frontiers and generations of people, and that it must allow for the inevitable changes that take place in the forests, in needs, and hence in policy.

groups.desc:
Questions 34-39

Look at the following statements issued by the conference.

Which six of the following statements, A-J, refer to the resolutions that were issued?
Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.

list.text: All kinds of species of trees should be preserved.
Fragile mountain forests should be given priority in research programs.
The surviving natural forests of Europe do not need priority treatment.
Research is to be better co-ordinated throughout Europe.
Information on forest fires should be collected and shared.
Loss of leaves from trees should be more extensively and carefully monitored.
Resources should be allocated to research into tree diseases.
Skiing should be encouraged in thinly populated areas.
Soil imbalances such as acidification should be treated with compounds of nitrogen and sulphur.
Information is to be systematically gathered on any decline in the condition of forests.

questions.title: Resolution 1
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Resolution 1" 需要与待匹配项中的某一陈述对应。文章中提到 "The first proposes the extension and systematisation of surveillance sites to monitor forest decline." 这句话与选项 J "Information is to be systematically gathered on any decline in the condition of forests" 最为匹配。这里的 "systematisation of surveillance sites" 与 "systematically gathered" 表达了相同的意思，而 "monitor forest decline" 与 "decline in the condition of forests" 也是一致的。因此，选项 J 是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: The first proposes the extension and systematisation of surveillance sites to monitor forest decline.
central_sentences.translation: 第一项决议提出扩展和系统化监测点以监控森林衰退。

target_sen.target_sen: The first proposes the extension and systematisation of surveillance sites to monitor forest decline.

questions.title: Resolution 2
questions.analysis: 第二项决议的内容是保护欧洲森林的基因多样性，目标是逆转树种数量的减少，或者至少保存所有树种的“遗传物质”。选项A的陈述“所有种类的树木都应该被保存”与此内容完全一致，因此选项A是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: The second resolution concentrates on the need to preserve the genetic diversity of European forests. The aim is to reverse the decline in the number of tree species or at least to preserve the 'genetic material' of all of them.
central_sentences.translation: 第二项决议集中在保护欧洲森林的基因多样性的需求上。其目标是扭转树种数量的下降趋势，或者至少保存所有树种的"遗传物质"。

target_sen.target_sen: The second resolution concentrates on the need to preserve the genetic diversity of European forests.

questions.title: Resolution 3
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Resolution 3" 需要匹配的陈述是关于森林火灾的信息收集和共享。文章中提到的 "establishment of a European databank on the subject" 和 "All information used in the development of national preventative policies would become generally available" 与题干中的 "Information on forest fires should be collected and shared" 是同义表达。因此，正确答案是 E。

central_sentences.sentence: Although forest fires do not affect all of Europe to the same extent, the amount of damage caused the experts to propose as the third resolution that the Strasbourg conference consider the establishment of a European databank on the subject. All information used in the development of national preventative policies would become generally available.
central_sentences.translation: 尽管森林火灾对欧洲各地的影响程度不同，但由于其造成的损害，专家们提议将建立一个关于这个主题的欧洲数据库作为斯特拉斯堡会议的第三项决议。所有用于制定国家预防政策的信息将普遍可用。

questions.title: Resolution 4
questions.analysis: 第四个决议的主题是山地森林，文章明确提到 "Proposed developments include a preferential research program on mountain forests"，这与选项 B "Fragile mountain forests should be given priority in research programs" 是同义表达。因此，选项 B 是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Proposed developments include a preferential research program on mountain forests.
central_sentences.translation: 拟议的发展包括对山地森林的优先研究计划。

syn_ary.synonym: The subject of the fourth resolution

target_sen.target_sen: The subject of the fourth resolution discussed by the ministers was mountain forests.

questions.title: Resolution 5
questions.analysis: Resolution 5的正确答案是G，因为文章明确提到第五项决议是关于分配资源进行树木疾病研究的。通过识别文章和题干中的关键词，并排除不相关的选项，可以确定答案。

central_sentences.sentence: The fifth resolution relaunched the European research network on the physiology of trees, called Eurosilva. Eurosilva should support joint European research on tree diseases and their physiological and biochemical aspects. Each country concerned could increase the number of scholarships and other financial support for doctoral theses and research projects in this area.
central_sentences.translation: 第五项决议重新启动了名为Eurosilva的欧洲树木生理学研究网络。Eurosilva应支持关于树木疾病及其生理和生化方面的联合欧洲研究。每个相关国家可以增加奖学金数量和其他财政支持，用于这一领域的博士论文和研究项目。

target_sen.target_sen: The fifth resolution relaunched the European research network on the physiology of trees, called Eurosilva.

questions.title: Resolution 6
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 D，因为文章中明确提到斯特拉斯堡会议建立了一个欧洲研究网络框架，并且提到要协调各个国家的活动，这与选项 D 中的“Research is to be better co-ordinated throughout Europe”意思相同。

central_sentences.sentence: Finally, the conference established the framework for a European research network on forest ecosystems. This would also involve harmonising activities in individual countries as well as identifying a number of priority research topics relating to the protection of forests.
central_sentences.translation: 最后，会议建立了一个关于森林生态系统的欧洲研究网络框架。这将涉及协调各个国家的活动，以及确定一些与森林保护相关的优先研究主题。

questions.title: What is the best title for Reading Passage 3?

options.text: The biological, economic and recreational role of forests
Plans to protect the forests of Europe
The priority of European research into ecosystems
Proposals for a world-wide policy on forest management

questions.analysis: 文章的主要内容是关于保护欧洲森林的计划和措施。文章多次提到保护欧洲森林的计划和措施，例如“Strasbourg hosted the first Ministerial Conference on the protection of Europe's forests.” 以及“a central place in any ecologically coherent forest policy must be given to continuity over time and to the possible effects of unforeseen events, to ensure that the full potential of these forests is maintained.” 这些内容都表明文章的主要内容是关于保护欧洲森林的计划。因此，正确答案是B: Plans to protect the forests of Europe。

central_sentences.sentence: In December 1990, Strasbourg hosted the first Ministerial Conference on the protection of Europe's forests.
central_sentences.translation: 1990年12月，斯特拉斯堡举办了第一次关于保护欧洲森林的部长级会议。
